frame_analyses.description: Instructor (Antonio Webb, MD) in operating room setting, introducing the task. Training pad visible on table below with marked incision lines and suture block with Penrose drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational setup frame showing context and equipment

frame_analyses.description: Needle driver grasping needle at approximately 2/3 point from tip. Right hand operating the driver. Needle oriented for suture passage through drain marks.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good needle grasp position, appropriate angle for intracorporeal driving

frame_analyses.description: Needle beginning passage through first mark on drain. Entry angle appears perpendicular to drain surface. Left hand stabilizing drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean, controlled entry. Good tissue handling technique.

frame_analyses.description: Needle exiting through second mark on drain. Suture line visible between entry and exit points. Tension controlled.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Entry and exit points appear well-aligned with marked targets. Minimal deviation observed.

frame_analyses.description: Suture now fully passed through drain. Both ends visible with adequate length for knot tying. Drain position stable.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Successful placement. Suture tension maintained without tissue deformation.

frame_analyses.description: Right hand initiating first throw (surgeon's knot double wrap). Suture wrapped around needle driver in right hand. Left hand stabilizing suture with opposite driver.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean double wrap initiated. Right hand leads first throw as per standard technique.

frame_analyses.description: Right hand completing double wrap of surgeon's knot. Knot beginning to form. Tension applied gradually.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth wrap execution. Appropriate tension control. Double throw clearly visible.

frame_analyses.description: First throw (surgeon's knot) completed and cinched. Knot sits on drain at intended closure site. Both suture tails extending clearly.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Secure knot formation. Good positioning relative to drain marks.

frame_analyses.description: Left hand now leading second throw (single throw). Hand switch from first throw clearly demonstrated. Suture wrapped around left needle driver.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean hand alternation. Left hand leads single throw appropriately.

frame_analyses.description: Second throw (single) cinched and secured. Knot stack building appropriately. Suture tension maintained.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper single throw execution. Knot security appears good.

frame_analyses.description: Right hand initiating third throw (single throw). Hand switch from second throw executed. Suture wrapped around right needle driver.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Correct hand alternation. Right hand leads third single throw.

frame_analyses.description: Third throw (single) cinched and secured. Final knot stack complete with three throws (2-1-1 pattern). Knot sits firmly on drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Complete knot sequence executed. Stack appears secure and well-positioned.

frame_analyses.description: Laparoscopic scissors introduced. Right suture tail being cut close to knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Scissors positioned for clean cut. Appropriate cutting technique.

frame_analyses.description: Right suture tail cut. Left suture tail now being positioned for cutting.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First tail cut cleanly. Repositioning for second tail cut.

frame_analyses.description: Both suture tails cut. Task complete. Knot remains secure on drain with both ends inside trainer. Drain slit appears fully closed.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean completion. All suture ends cut inside trainer as required.

penalties.description: Entry point approximately 0.5mm from mark, exit point approximately 1mm from mark. Total deviation: 1.5mm

knot_assessments.notes: Clean double wrap with appropriate tension. Knot sits precisely on drain at intended location.
Correct hand switch from throw 1. Single throw executed cleanly. Knot stack building appropriately.
Proper alternation from throw 2. Final single throw secure. Complete 2-1-1 knot sequence.

confidence_rationale: Expert demonstration with clear visibility of all key steps. Needle entry/exit angles well-visualized. All three throws clearly demonstrated with proper hand switching. Knot security and drain closure assessment confident. Minor uncertainty (±0.5mm) on exact suture deviation due to camera angle during entry, but overall assessment highly reliable.
technique_summary: Excellent expert-level performance demonstrating proficiency well above the 112-second benchmark. All task requirements executed with precision: proper needle loading and positioning, accurate suture placement through marked targets, correct surgeon's knot (2-1-1) sequence with precise hand alternation between throws, secure knot formation, and clean suture tail cutting. Total time 74 seconds reflects expert skill level. Drain remains intact with complete slit closure and minimal suture deviation.
strengths: Rapid needle loading and orientation (5s vs 3-8s expert range)
Accurate suture placement with minimal deviation (<1.5mm total)
Clean, controlled double wrap for surgeon's knot with appropriate tension
Precise hand alternation across all three throws (2-1-1 pattern)
Secure knot positioning directly on drain at intended closure site
Efficient final suture cutting with both ends inside trainer
No tissue trauma or drain deformation throughout

demonstrated_techniques: Proper needle driver grasp at 2/3 point for optimal control
Perpendicular needle entry angle for clean tissue passage
Controlled wrist rotation for intracorporeal needle driving
Surgeon's knot (double throw) construction with right hand lead
Single throw execution with alternating hand leads
Hand exchange between throws maintaining suture control
Knot positioning directly over intended closure site
Appropriate tension application to avoid tissue damage
Scissors positioning for clean suture tail cutting
Completion with both tails cut inside trainer box
verbal_cues: Implicit demonstration of sequential throw execution without narration
common_errors_addressed: Avoiding oblique needle entry that would increase suture deviation
Maintaining proper hand positioning to enable smooth alternation
Preventing excessive tension that could deform drain or cause avulsion
Ensuring knot stack remains centered on intended closure site
Controlling scissors approach to avoid accidental tissue contact

improvement_suggestions: No meaningful improvements - performance meets expert proficiency standards
